eco_baseline_footprint.formula: BIF = (vCPU x 15 EUR) + (Storage GB x 0.08 EUR)
eco_baseline_footprint.method_note: RAM is considered included in compute cost and is not monetized separately in the operationalized framework model. This value refers strictly to the framework MCC and does not attempt to reproduce the full operational redundancy of the Avalanche validator network.
eco_baseline_footprint.source: Framework cost model applied to Avalanche Minimum Correctness Configuration (1 validator-class node with full C-Chain state replica)

ps_throughput.estimation_note: Conservative benchmark-level operational estimate for Avalanche C-Chain derived from documented gas-target capacity rather than from protocol-maximum or stress-benchmark claims.
ps_throughput.source: Avalanche Builder Hub, Avalanche C-Chain throughput / gas target documentation

eco_variable_transaction_cost.conversion_note: Representative benchmark-period average fee for ordinary Avalanche C-Chain transactions. The value is intended as an operational reference level rather than as a fixed protocol tariff and may vary with gas consumption, transaction type, and network conditions.
eco_variable_transaction_cost.source: Avalanche Support, transaction fees on Avalanche

eco_storage_footprint.source: Avalanche validator-class node system requirements / framework MCC normalization

parallel_execution.motivation: Within the adopted benchmark baseline, the C-Chain executes transactions against a globally shared EVM state and does not provide native parallel execution of independent transactions at the application layer.

horizontal_partitioning.motivation: The adopted C-Chain baseline does not rely on native horizontal partitioning or sharding of the ledger state.

absence_of_global_serialization.motivation: Avalanche does not rely on a Nakamoto-style longest-chain process; consensus is achieved through repeated validator sampling and metastable agreement rather than through globally serialized proof-of-work chain growth.

scalable_coordination_model.motivation: The Avalanche consensus family is designed to reduce coordination overhead compared with classical globally synchronized block-production models by relying on repeated subsampled validator interaction rather than on a single miner or leader bottleneck.

ps_scalability.source: Avalanche C-Chain architecture and Avalanche consensus model

append_only_structural_history.motivation: The C-Chain maintains an append-only blockchain history of committed blocks.

cryptographic_linkage_between_records.motivation: Blocks and state transitions are cryptographically linked in the native blockchain structure.

cryptographic_write_authentication.motivation: Transactions are authenticated by cryptographic signatures in the EVM account model.

independent_integrity_verification.motivation: Full nodes and validators can independently verify chain history and state validity.

rci_tamper_evidence_auditability.source: Avalanche C-Chain transaction and node model

protocol_enforced_irreversible_commit.motivation: Avalanche finality is very fast but not based on a deterministic certificate-style irreversible commit in the strict classical-BFT sense used by the framework.

multi_party_confirmation_requirement.motivation: Consensus depends on repeated confirmation across multiple validators under the Avalanche protocol.

absence_of_unilateral_rollback_capabilit.motivation: No single validator can unilaterally revert accepted state under the native protocol model.

absence_of_single_writer_requirement.motivation: Avalanche does not structurally depend on a single privileged writer.

quorum_based_write_continuation.motivation: Write continuation depends on distributed validator participation in the native consensus model rather than on a single coordinator.

automatic_write_continuation_mechanism.motivation: The protocol continues operation without relying on centralized manual failover.

multi_node_write_survivability.motivation: System continuity depends on the validator network rather than on any individual validator.

absence_of_single_entity_unilateral_stat.motivation: The Avalanche model does not assign structural unilateral authority over valid state to a single entity.

structural_requirement_for_multi_entity_.motivation: Validation and state acceptance depend on multiple validators in the native protocol model.

validator_admission_independence.motivation: Validator participation is staking-based and does not require discretionary admission by a central enterprise authority.

absence_of_structurally_fixed_validator_.motivation: The validator set is not structurally closed or fixed.

native_privilege_differentiation_capabil.motivation: Avalanche does not implement a native enterprise identity and privilege governance framework comparable to RBAC systems.

native_identity_lifecycle_governance_cap.motivation: There is no native protocol-level identity lifecycle governance comparable to permissioned enterprise platforms.

structural_independence_of_identity_gove.motivation: The protocol does not provide a native identity and access governance framework in the sense required by this criterion.

non_unilateral_compromise_requirement.motivation: A single validator cannot unilaterally compromise the system in the native Avalanche operational model.

majority_compromise_required.motivation: Reliable compromise requires coordinated control of a substantial fraction of validation power.

supermajority_compromise_required.motivation: The model does not rely on a classical BFT-style supermajority certificate threshold.
